text: The Survey of Health, Ageing and Retirement in Europe (SHARE) provides an opportunity to address some of the many pressing societal problems that arise in connection with the rapidly ageing population in Europe. This includes a whole range of questions: How do Europeans experience the process of ageing? What are the characteristics of the economic, social and health processes that these individuals go through in the course of their lives, and how do the different processes influence each other? This multidisciplinary volume on social inclusion and multidimensional deprivation using the latest longitudinal data from the 5th wave of SHARE will contribute to answering these and other questions and will thus be a highly useful reference source for policymakers as well as academics. Through an in-depth examination of the links between health, wealth and social relationships, this book provides timely research-based information that can be used in the design of policy responses that will support the realisation of an inclusive society for all ages.
